title: inconsistent exited state name b/w root and rootless mode with -d
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]: # **kind bug**

**Description**

rootless
```
$ podman run --name cont2 -d registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29 sleep 0.5 && sleep 1 && podman inspect --type container --format '{{ .State.Status }}' cont2 && podman rm cont2
dedc44898ca0552c2a17151f30db85d2038e82618aa85f896ab9bc98e2848860
stopped
dedc44898ca0552c2a17151f30db85d2038e82618aa85f896ab9bc98e2848860
```

root
```
# podman run --name cont2 -d registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29 sleep 0.5 && sleep 1 && podman inspect --type container --format '{{ .State.Status }}' cont2 && podman rm cont2
e4b93ea8a6fba8f5d75d107ecd9c4d291276225b8c931ead54c58c831aebaae7
exited
e4b93ea8a6fba8f5d75d107ecd9c4d291276225b8c931ead54c58c831aebaae7
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.11.1
Go Version:    go1.11
Git Commit:    "8967a1d691ed44896b81ad48c863033f23c65eb0-dirty"
Built:         Thu Nov  8 22:35:40 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
Rootless is not successfully running the postexit cleanup process.

@giuseppe It looks like the `podman cleanup` exit command we add to Conmon is failing silently - I'm not getting any debug output from it. Might be something about execing rootless Podman straight from Conmon?

this is podman believing to run in root mode.  We need to add some env variables to the conmon process so that once podman is called, it can understand we are in a user namespace.

PR here: https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1861
